title: Neurogramma delavayi
taxonomy: Neurogramma delavayi is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It is a member of the genus Neurogramma, which is a group of plants endemic to China. The plant is further classified as a type of epiphyte, meaning it grows on other plants or surfaces, typically in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Neurogramma delavayi is based on its unique characteristics, such as its leaf shape and arrangement. The plant's classification is also influenced by its genetic relationships with other members of the Gesneriaceae family.

intro: Neurogramma delavayi is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Gesneriaceae, commonly found in the mountainous regions of China. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Neurogramma delavayi is rich in bioactive constituents, including iridoid glycosides, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, extracts, and infusions, which are commonly consumed to alleviate respiratory issues and boost the immune system.
